blockingIssues.description: The vendored `just-bash` smoke test puts its type-only assertions in `packages/webapp/tests/vendor/just-bash-smoke.test.ts`, but `npm run typecheck` does not compile test files, so CI never validates the promised `import { parse, type ScriptNode } from 'just-bash'` type surface.
The AST-backed allow-list walker does not inspect `SimpleCommand.assignments`, so assignment values like `FOO=$(whoami) ls` can smuggle command substitution under an allow-listed head.

The AST-backed allow-list walker only recurses through `DoubleQuoted` parts and misses nested executable words under parameter-expansion defaults/assignments and brace-expansion items, leaving additional substitution bypasses under an allow-listed head.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/environment.md` to note that `npm run typecheck` only runs the repo's three production `tsconfig` entry points and does not compile Vitest `.test.ts` files, so compile-time assertions placed only in tests are not CI-backed by the TypeScript gate.

Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` with just-bash AST gotchas for allow-list work: `&&` chains remain within one `StatementNode`, and executable nested words can appear in assignments, parameter-expansion defaults/assignments, and brace-expansion items.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a vendoring feature promises CI-backed type-surface coverage, require the compile-time assertion to live on a `tsc`-covered source path or a dedicated typecheck target that `npm run typecheck` actually compiles; `.test.ts`-only assertions are not enough.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `vendor-just-bash-with-parse-exposed` placed its type-only smoke assertions in `packages/webapp/tests/vendor/just-bash-smoke.test.ts`, but `npm run typecheck` only runs `tsc --noEmit` on `tsconfig.cli.json`, `tsconfig.json`, and `tsconfig.worker.json`, whose include globs exclude test files.

rejectedObservations.observation: Clarify the mission-wide live-browser/CDP requirement for pure utility-wrapper features.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate: round 2 already captured the same systemic guidance conflict in `suggestedGuidanceUpdates`, so repeating it here would not add new evidence.
